$comment: Managed settings template for fleet deployment. Place at: /Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/managed-settings.json (macOS), /etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json (Linux/WSL), or C:\Program Files\ClaudeCode\managed-settings.json (Windows). Managed settings cannot be overridden by users or projects. Replace <ORG-MARKETPLACE-URL> with your internal marketplace git URL or path.
$comment_allowManagedHooksOnly: Set allowManagedHooksOnly to true for maximum lockdown: only hooks from managed settings and managed plugins run, so users cannot add hooks that interfere with the guardrails. Test with your developers' existing hooks first.

env.$comment_env: Fleet-wide guardrail tuning. AGW_LEVEL picks an enforcement bundle; the default (omit it) is 'standard'. Levels: 'strict' (archive even node_modules, no session-approval memory), 'standard' (safe default), 'relaxed' (credential reads allowed without prompting, still audited), 'observe' (shadow mode: log only, block nothing — use to trial the plugin without disrupting work). Individual knobs (AGW_SESSION_MEMORY, AGW_REGENERABLE_RM, AGW_RELAXED_ACCESS, AGW_ENFORCEMENT) override the level. AGW_ARCHIVE_MAX_BYTES caps the archive store (0 = unlimited, the safe default); oldest pre-image snapshots are evicted first, never move-archives or the newest version.
env.$comment_AGW_HOME: The machine-wide archive/session store. Defaults to ~/.agw. Point it at a persistent, NON-cloud-synced path. IMPORTANT for Cowork: hooks run in a per-session Linux VM whose home (~) is wiped on teardown, so ~/.agw does not persist there — set AGW_HOME to a mounted persistent volume.

$comment_optional_hardening: Optionally also pin permissions at the managed level, e.g.: "permissions": { "deny": ["Bash(rm:*)", "Bash(sudo:*)"] } — the plugin's hooks already cover these with better redirects, but managed permission rules add defense in depth that survives even plugin removal.
